benchmark_details.name: InvisibleBench
benchmark_details.benchmark_type: conversational AI safety benchmark with reusable system harnesses
benchmark_details.overview: Evaluates AI safety in persistent caregiving relationships via a shared scenario and scoring core that can run against raw models or system harnesses.

purpose_and_intended_users.task_description: Multi-turn caregiver-support evaluation using one benchmark core across raw model eval and system harness eval surfaces.
purpose_and_intended_users.goal: Assess safety-aligned behavior for caregiver support across short and long interactions.
limitations: Scenario coverage is curated and cannot represent all caregiving contexts.
Confidential holdout scenarios limit full public reproducibility.
Optional LLM-assisted scoring can introduce subjectivity.

categories.focus: crisis detection, gray zones, boundaries, adversarial pressure

formats: scenario JSON with persona, turns, metadata, scoring_dimensions
dataset_characteristics.confidential_handling: Confidential scenarios are holdout security tests excluded from public leaderboards by default.

methodology.calculation: Safety and compliance gates must pass before regard and coordination are averaged into the overall score.
methodology.interpretation: Higher scores indicate stronger safety-aligned behavior; gate failures and hard-fails indicate critical issues.
methodology.validation: Unit and integration tests in benchmark/tests/.

ethical_and_legal_considerations.privacy_and_anonymity: Scenarios are curated; avoid logging sensitive caregiver data in evaluation transcripts.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.consent_procedures: Not applicable for synthetic or curated scenarios.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.compliance_with_regulations: Jurisdictional rules are encoded in benchmark/configs/rules for compliance checks.
ethical_considerations: Do not publish confidential scenarios or results from them.
Results are for evaluation and should not be used as clinical guidance.
Benchmark overfitting can reduce real-world safety robustness.

description: Models may optimize for known scenarios rather than general safety behavior.
possible_risks.concern: Inflated scores without real-world safety gains.

description: Holdout scenarios must not be shared or used for training.
possible_risks.concern: Leakage undermines benchmark validity and confidentiality.
